questions.prompt: A team's upgrades don't charge until the next invoice. Customer upgrades to Premium but doesn't see Premium features until next month. Right fix?

choices.text: Manually grant features and figure out billing later.

Use `proration_behavior: 'always_invoice'` when updating the subscription — Stripe creates and pays a proration invoice immediately. Customer is charged the difference now and gets features now. Match billing to feature access.

questions.explanation: Default `create_prorations` puts the proration on the next invoice. For instant-feature upgrades, switch to `always_invoice` so the prorated amount bills immediately. Aligns 'paid now' with 'gets features now.'

questions.prompt: Why collect a card during free trial signup instead of at trial end?

choices.text: Trial-to-paid conversion is dramatically higher when the card is already on file. Without it: every user must come back, find the page, enter card details — many never do. With card on file at start (via SetupIntent or initial subscription), conversion is automatic when trial ends.

questions.explanation: Card-at-trial-start vs. card-at-trial-end is one of the highest-leverage subscription decisions. Conversion typically 2-3× higher with card on file. The mild signup friction is offset by removing the much bigger trial-to-paid friction.
